content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed all 3 changed files (br_models.rs, project.rs, cli.rs). The changes are well-structured: (1) skip_serializing_if on DependencyRef.status prevents null in JSON output, (2) map_br_list_error extraction adds smarter corrupt-data detection via stderr keyword matching, (3) compact_planned_elsewhere_summary gains scope-label stripping, heading filtering, and list-marker handling to extract the body text from structured descriptions. All 8 new tests pass. The code is deterministic, pure, and correctly handles edge cases like fenced code blocks within descriptions. No security concerns, no correctness bugs, no significant robustness gaps found.

## Amendments

1. In `compact_planned_elsewhere_summary`, a line containing a colon where the left-hand side happens to match a scope label (e.g., "Summary: this is actual content about a summary feature") will have its left portion silently stripped. Consider requiring that the label appears at the start of a new logical block (e.g., preceded by a blank line or heading) or that it is the first non-blank line, rather than matching any line that begins with a recognized label followed by a colon. Currently the function already returns on the first substantive line, so this only matters if the scope label line IS the first substantive line — which limits the blast radius, but the ambiguity still exists for descriptions like "Summary: Build a summary export pipeline" where "Summary" is a scope label but also part of the feature name.
   Rationale: The scope-label stripping is positional (first substantive line) and keyword-based, which creates an ambiguity when a real content line starts with a recognized scope word followed by a colon. The risk is low because this only affects the first non-blank, non-fence, non-heading line, but a false positive would silently truncate the planned-elsewhere summary.
